comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "All 4 children (524d, 7522, 2711, 9ed2) are closed with their own completion-verifier signatures, so the epic's child-closure guard is satisfied and every unit was independently certified. The epic-level completion verifier could not run: its decide@then/verify step deterministically emitted un-parseable prose twice ('output could not be parsed even after repair') — a verifier output-format infra failure, not a FAIL verdict (the captured evidence showed AC1..ACn met). Force-closing to work around the parser bug; the change stack (Gerrit 287-290) is LLM-Review+MAX and Verified+MAX.". Session: bbfa1c1d9.

compiled_state.description:
## Context

The Jira reconciler coordinates concurrent passes across clones (local + the GitHub-Actions
reconcile bridge) with a **pass-lock** and a **phase-gate**. Today both are **working-tree files
committed to the shared `tickets` orphan branch**: `.reconciler-pass-lock` and
`.reconciler-phase-gate` (`_advisory_lock.py:69-70`), written by `acquire_pass_lock`
(`_advisory_lock.py:243`), released by `release_pass_lock` (`:302`), orchestrated in
`rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:373-447`. The body is `pass_id\ntimestamp_ns`.

Two problems with a tree-committed lock:

1. **It rides the `merge=ours` band-aid.** `.reconciler-*` is matched by the `merge=ours` driver
   in the tickets-branch `.gitattributes` (`_commands/init.py:57`; `.bridge_state/*` is line 56).
   Two clones that touch the lock on diverged histories reconverge by silently keeping one side.
2. **No self-healing.** No lease/heartbeat/steal. A pass that **crashes while holding the lock**
   leaves a stale file that clears only on the next clean run. The current mitigation is the
   `b859-8fa1` **retry band-aid** (`_advisory_lock.py:73-89, 263-291`).

**Recent ground truth.** `feat/reconcile-continuous-loop` is **merged to main** (flag-gated on the
`RECONCILE_CONTINUOUS` GitHub Variable, default off) and explicitly relies on "concurrency + the
reconciler pass-lock" to prevent overlapping passes — it is the contention amplifier this epic
hardens against.

This epic is the **WS-2 (coordination) half** of the former "conflict-free reconciler state" epic;
WS-1 (event-sourcing the binding *data*) was dropped because the closed epic *Level-triggered
bridge convergence* (`3006-e198-13db-4e1f`, ADRs 0026-0029 + the `8de5` classifier/canary) already
made binding drift detected and self-healing and `bindings.json` is a derived cache.

**Framed as an EFFICIENCY lock, not a correctness lock.** Reconcile passes are idempotent; the
lock avoids wasted overlapping work. A rare double-acquire degrades to two idempotent passes
(tolerated by the `3006` `classify()` matrix + acting-fraction breaker), never to corruption.

## Design (WS-2 — self-healing ref-lock)

- Move pass-lock and phase-gate OFF the working tree into **bare git refs** `refs/reconciler/lock`
  and `refs/reconciler/gate` (ref -> blob), so they are never in the tickets tree, never
  union-merged, never subject to `merge=ours`. Authoritative on `origin` (the `sync.remote`).
- **Blob = newline-terminated UTF-8 JSON** `{holder, lease_secs, heartbeat_ns, fence}`. Decode of
  a corrupt/partial blob **fails closed** (a contender that cannot parse treats the lock as HELD,
  never free). Clock source is plain `time.time_ns()` — **not** `rebar._store.hlc` (that fcntl
  cache is for ticket-event ordering, the wrong tool for a lease).
- **Blob creation:** `git hash-object -w --stdin` to plant the blob, then a **CAS** on the ref:
  `git update-ref <ref> <new-oid> <old>` (acquire old = 40 zeros = create-only; release/steal old =
  the exact observed oid). Reuse the existing CAS retry helper `_cas_advance_with_retry`
  (`_advisory_lock.py:447-487`); its discriminator `_is_cas_mismatch` (`:415`, line 425) hard-codes
  `refs/heads/tickets` and must be generalized to accept the ref name.
- **Relative-duration, skew-proof lease (DynamoDB-lock-client pattern):** a contender does NOT
  compare clocks across clones. It records `(observed_oid, fence)`, waits one lease interval on its
  OWN clock, re-reads; if `fence` and oid are unchanged, the holder made no progress for a full
  lease -> dead -> **CAS-break against `observed_oid`**. The CAS guarantees a double-break race
  resolves to **exactly one winner** (the loser observes CAS failure and does NOT also acquire).
  `fence` is the progress witness + fencing token, bumped on every acquire/renew/steal.
- **Lease + heartbeat:** default lease **120 s**, heartbeat every **~lease/3**, both configurable
  via a new `[reconciler]` config key `lock_lease_secs` (which **replaces** the retired
  `lock_max_retries`). Because a pass can run 10-60 min, the holder renews from a **daemon thread**
  started on acquire; a renewal-CAS failure (`LeaseLostError`) sets a `lock_lost`
  `threading.Event` that the pass polls at per-ticket checkpoints and, when set, raises
  `ReconcileLockLost` to abort (idempotent -> safe re-run). Heartbeat git ops touch only
  `refs/reconciler/lock`, disjoint from tickets-branch writes.
- **Phase-gate** uses the **same** blob schema + CAS discipline as the lock (not operator-freeform).
- **Expand-contract cutover:** a `[reconciler] lock_backend = file | ref` switch lets the ref
  backend land alongside the file backend (C3) and the file backend + `merge=ours` + `b859` band-aid
  be removed only after the ref backend is proven (C4) — a clean rollback path.
- **Remote feasibility is load-bearing and unproven:** whether GitHub accepts push/fetch of a
  non-branch `refs/reconciler/*` ref pointing at a **blob** (with the CI `GITHUB_TOKEN`) is verified
  FIRST (C1 AC0); the contingency if it does not is ref -> tiny-commit instead of ref -> blob.

Out of scope: `.ticket-write.lock` (`_store/lock.py`, a local fcntl lock for single-host ticket
writes) — a separate mechanism, not cross-clone pass coordination. And all of WS-1 (binding data).

## Success Criteria

- The reconciler coordinates concurrent passes with **zero coordination state in the tickets
  working tree**, and a crashed pass-holder never wedges the reconciler beyond one lease interval.
- The `merge=ours` policy no longer covers any live coordination state (`.bridge_state/*` unchanged).
- The design + its rollback path are captured in an ADR.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pass-lock and phase-gate live in `refs/reconciler/*` (bare ref -> blob, not in the working
      tree, not union-merged); acquire is create-only CAS, release/steal is observed-oid CAS
      (`_engine/rebar_reconciler/_ref_lock.py` + `_advisory_lock.py` + `rebar_reconciler/__main__.py`).
- [ ] Blob is UTF-8 JSON `{holder, lease_secs, heartbeat_ns, fence}` (`time.time_ns()` clock);
      corrupt/partial decode fails closed (treated as HELD, never free).
- [ ] Stale-lock self-heal via relative-duration lease (no cross-clone clock compare): a contender
      CAS-breaks only after observing no `fence`/oid progress across one locally-measured lease; a
      double-break race resolves to exactly one winner (regression test). Holder renews at ~lease/3
      from a daemon thread; a lost-lease renewal aborts the pass.
- [ ] `lock_backend = file | ref` switch enables an expand-contract cutover; the ref-lock design +
      rollback path are recorded in a new ADR under `docs/adr/`.
- [ ] `.reconciler-*` removed from the `merge=ours` `.gitattributes` (`_commands/init.py:57`, via a
      migration that also updates already-initialized trackers) and from
      `tests/interfaces/store/test_gitattributes_merge.py`; `.bridge_state/*` left intact; no
      `.reconciler-*` file is committed to the tickets branch (regression test).
- [ ] The `b859-8fa1` retry band-aid (`_advisory_lock.py:73-89, 263-291`) and its now-dead helpers
      (`_resolve_retry_budget`, `_LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET_DEFAULT`, `_compute_backoff_seconds`) + config
      key `lock_max_retries` (`_config_schema.py:334,445`) + env aliases + their tests are removed.
- [ ] Reconcile-pass idempotency asserted by a named double-acquire convergence test; the existing
      ref-CAS primitive is reused (not duplicated). Full non-integration suite green.
compiled_state.title: Self-healing Jira-reconciler pass-lock: move pass-lock/phase-gate off the tickets branch into refs/reconciler/* (retire merge=ours + b859 retry band-aid)
